stories.headline: Iran suspends indirect US talks over Lebanon and vows to fully close the Strait of Hormuz; oil jumps
stories.summary: Iranian state media said Tehran suspended all mediated exchanges with Washington, citing Israel's Lebanon operations as a ceasefire violation 'on all fronts,' and resolved to completely close the Strait of Hormuz and activate the Bab al-Mandab front. Oil prices rose more than $6 a barrel after the announcement.

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran walks away from mediated US talks and threatens Hormuz, jolting oil markets and reviving escalation fears.
perspectives.summary: CNBC and CNN reported Tehran halted indirect negotiations and pledged to 'completely' block Hormuz, accusing Israel of breaking the ceasefire in Lebanon. The move sent oil up over $6 a barrel and tied Iran's nuclear and sanctions track to events in Lebanon and Gaza.

sources.title: Iran stops negotiations with U.S., vows to 'completely' block Strait of Hormuz: State media
Live updates: Iran suspends talks with US over Israel's strikes on Lebanon

perspectives.keyFraming: Tehran ties its nuclear and ceasefire diplomacy to Lebanon, suspending mediator channels amid Israel's Beirut escalation.
perspectives.summary: Euronews reported Iran suspended negotiations via mediators, with Tehran framing Lebanon as a precondition of the truce now violated and stressing the linkage of the nuclear file to the wider regional ceasefire.

sources.title: Iran suspended negotiations via mediators with US, state media says

stories.headline: Oil jumps as much as 8% as Iran reportedly halts US talks and threatens full Strait of Hormuz closure
stories.summary: Oil surged Monday after Iranian state media said Tehran will suspend message exchanges with Washington and completely block the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon. WTI rose toward the low-to-mid $90s and Brent toward the mid-to-high $90s. Treasuries fell as traders priced higher energy-driven inflation and reduced odds of Fed cuts.

perspectives.keyFraming: A diplomatic breakdown reignites a supply-shock premium; the bond market reads it as inflationary and Fed-hawkish.
perspectives.summary: CNBC reported WTI up more than 7% and Brent near the high $90s after Tehran's halt-and-block threat, with analysts flagging the risk of much higher prices if peace efforts fully collapse. Markets had been pricing an eventual deal.

sources.title: U.S. oil jumps more than 7% on report Iran will halt talks with U.S. and completely block Hormuz

perspectives.keyFraming: The energy shock is bleeding into rates as bonds slid on fears higher costs force the Fed to stay tight.
perspectives.summary: Bloomberg reported Treasuries sank, with the 10-year yield up about 8 basis points, as the US-Iran stall lifted oil more than 7%, raising concern energy costs will stoke inflation.

sources.title: US Bonds Slide as US-Iran Talks Stall, Sending Oil Prices Higher

stories.headline: Israel orders strikes on Beirut's Dahiyeh as Hezbollah rockets hit the north; civilians flee
stories.summary: Netanyahu and Defense Minister Katz ordered the IDF to strike Hezbollah targets in Beirut's southern suburbs after rocket fire toward northern Israel, citing repeated ceasefire violations. Thousands fled Dahiyeh amid gridlock and flight cancellations, a day after Israel captured the 900-year-old Beaufort Castle.

perspectives.keyFraming: Netanyahu and Katz vow no Hezbollah asset is off-limits after the group broke the truce.
perspectives.summary: Israeli officials framed the operation as a necessary response to Hezbollah aggression, vowing any threat to Israeli cities will cost the group strategic assets one after another, as the IDF captured Beaufort Castle and pushed toward the Litani.

sources.title: Israeli PM Netanyahu orders strikes on Hezbollah in Beirut; IDF captures Beaufort Castle

perspectives.keyFraming: Lebanese pour out of Dahiyeh fearing a return of Israeli bombardment as diplomacy falters.
perspectives.summary: Al Jazeera reported residents fleeing Beirut's southern suburbs after Israel announced impending strikes, with airlines cancelling flights and fears of renewed occupation, while Lebanon appealed to the US to restrain Israel.

sources.title: Lebanese start fleeing south Beirut after Israel says will target the area

stories.headline: Nvidia enters the PC market with an Arm-based 'RTX Spark' superchip at Computex; rival chipmakers slide
stories.summary: At Computex 2026, Nvidia's Jensen Huang unveiled the Arm-based 'RTX Spark' superchip — an Arm CPU paired with a Blackwell GPU and 128GB of unified memory — to power AI-focused Windows PCs from Microsoft, Dell, HP and Lenovo. Nvidia rose about 5% and Arm jumped, while Qualcomm, Intel and AMD fell on PC-competition fears.

perspectives.keyFraming: A structural land-grab: Nvidia muscles into Intel, AMD and Qualcomm's PC turf with an Arm-plus-Blackwell platform.
perspectives.summary: CNBC reported Nvidia jumping into PCs with a new Arm-based chip debuting in laptops from Microsoft, Dell and HP, its first serious push into the client-CPU market. Yahoo Finance reported NVDA rallying about 5% while Qualcomm fell sharply on AI-PC competition fears.

sources.title: Nvidia jumps into PCs with new Arm-based chip debuting in laptops from Microsoft, Dell, HP
NVIDIA Rallies 5% on RTX Spark Launch as Qualcomm Falls 7% on AI PC Competition Fears

stories.headline: US and Iran trade fresh strikes as a peace deal stalls; Trump blasts critics in an overnight post
stories.summary: The US said it struck Iranian radar and drone sites over the weekend in self-defense after Iran downed a US drone, and Iran said it hit a US-linked air base, as a 13-week war continued amid stalled talks and an unsigned ceasefire-extension memorandum. In an overnight Truth Social post, Trump attacked Democratic and Republican critics for complicating negotiations.

perspectives.keyFraming: A peace deal Trump has repeatedly called imminent remains elusive as both sides keep trading fire.
perspectives.summary: CNBC and TIME reported the US and Iran exchanged strikes over the weekend even as officials say a ceasefire-extension memorandum is largely negotiated. Trump insists Tehran 'really wants' a deal, while Iranian state media say his added demands contradict the text.

sources.title: Trump hits out at 'chirping' critics as U.S. and Iran launch fresh wave of strikes
U.S. and Iran Exchange Renewed Fire as Trump Claims 'All Will Work Out Well'

perspectives.keyFraming: An erratic, late-night presidential post underscores a stalled war that most Americans oppose.
perspectives.summary: Left-leaning outlets foregrounded Trump's roughly 1 a.m. post raging at 'unpatriotic Republicans' and 'Dumocrats' as evidence of an unsteady commander-in-chief presiding over an unpopular war he launched, noting rising costs and low approval.

sources.title: Manic Trump, 79, Melts Down at 'Unpatriotic' Republicans in 1AM Ragepost
